mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) reflect expanding output and higher corporate earnings, which drive positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases month-over-month but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration, reducing consumer income and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns in the following 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over the last 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by >0.3pp over 3 months but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such events over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of below-trend GDP growth (GDPC1) and a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals that monetary policy is accommodative relative to economic slack, foreshadowing future growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the interaction term (low FEDFUNDS × low GDP growth) fails to produce a Rank IC > 0.04 over any 3-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: During economic expansions (real GDP growth above 2% year-over-year), rising real retail sales (RSAFS) confirm strong consumer demand, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If in periods with GDPC1 growth >2% year-over-year, rising RSAFS leads to negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 40% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 10% signals a strong rebound in residential investment, a leading sector for economic activity, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 10% month-over-month
failure_condition: If HOUST rises >10% m/m but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of cases over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: The divergence between rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) and stagnant industrial production (INDPRO) signals labor hoarding and falling productivity, which compresses corporate margins and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the ratio of PAYEMS to INDPRO increases but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation, measured by the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL), erodes real corporate earnings and increases discount rates, resulting in lower equity valuations and negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL increases month-over-month but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 60% of occurrences over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the federal funds rate by at least 25 basis points in a single month surprises financial markets and triggers a repricing of equities downward, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 25 basis points month-over-month
failure_condition: If after a 25bp+ FEDFUNDS increase, S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of cases over a 3-year period.
